英國英格蘭坎布利其爾波迪夏馬
zh.lifeSummary: Joane de Burgh於1370年出生在英國坎布利其爾。她於大約1393年與William Alington在英國坎布利其爾波迪夏馬結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1429年5月15日在英國林可爾史爾福拉比享年59歲，埋葬在英國坎布利其爾波迪夏馬。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joane de Burgh于1370年出生在英国坎布利其爾。她于大约1393年与William Alington在英国坎布利其爾波迪夏馬结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1429年5月15日在英国林可爾史爾福拉比享年59岁，埋葬在英国坎布利其爾波迪夏馬。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Joane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jean .

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Joane de Burgh was born in 1370, in Cambridgeshire, England. She married William Alington about 1393, in Bottisham, Cambridgeshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 15 May 1429, in Wrawby, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 59, and was buried in Bottisham, Cambridgeshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Joane de Burgh est née en 1370, à Cambridgeshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé William Alington environ 1393, à Bottisham, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée le 15 mai 1429, à Wrawby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterrée à Bottisham, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Joane de Burgh wurde 1370 in Cambridgeshire, England geboren. Sie hat um 1393 in Bottisham, Cambridgeshire, England William Alington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb am 15. Mai 1429 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Wrawby, Lincolnshire, England und wurde in Bottisham, Cambridgeshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Joane de Burgh è nata il nel 1370 nel Cambridgeshire, England. Lei sposato William Alington circa 1393, nel Bottisham, Cambridgeshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 15 maggio 1429, nel Wrawby, Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolta a Bottisham, Cambridgeshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joane de Burghは1370年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアで生まれた。約1393年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアボッティシャムでWilliam Alingtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1429年5月15日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアウラウビーで59歳で死去し，イギリスカンブリジェシュアイアボッティシャムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joane de Burgh(은)는 1370년 영국 캄브리지실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1393년 영국 캄브리지실르 벗티샤에서 William Alington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1429년 5월 15일 영국 링코실르 롤비에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 캄브리지실르 벗티샤에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Joane de Burgh nasceu em 1370, em Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com William Alington aproximadamente 1393, em Bottisham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 15 de maio de 1429, em Wrawby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 59 anos, e foi sepultada em Bottisham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Joane de Burgh родилась в 1370 г. в местности камбриджешир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Alington около 1393 г. в местности боттишам, камбриджешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла 15 мая 1429 г. в местности равби, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 59 лет и была похоронена в местности боттишам, камбриджешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Joane de Burgh nació en 1370, en Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con William Alington aproximadamente 1393, en Bottisham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 15 de mayo de 1429, en Wrawby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultada en Bottisham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra.
